overview: This foliose lichen appears to be Lobarina scrobiculata, also known as textured lungwort, which is characterized by its brownish, deeply pitted or "scrobiculate" surface. It is a cyanolichen, meaning it partners with cyanobacteria to fix nitrogen from the air. These lichens are commonly found on tree bark or mossy rocks in moist, shaded environments, particularly in old-growth forests. While generally secure, it is sensitive to acidifying air pollution and prefers areas with high atmospheric humidity. Wikipedia +3 4 sites Lobarina - Wikipedia
